ops.title: cc-steer main CI is chronically red from cc-transcript v10 mining API drift, not local bugs; watcher now launchd-supervised
ops.body:
cc-steer's GitHub Actions CI (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) clones cc-transcript's
**HEAD** (`git clone --depth 1 .../yasyf/cc-transcript ../cc-transcript` — the
"unreleased v10 path dep"). As of 2026-07-11, cc-transcript main dropped
`cc_transcript.mining.sample_windows` and the `cc_transcript.mining.sampling`
module, so 5 cc-steer test modules (test_cli_sync/daemon/negatives/pipeline/shadow)
**fail at pytest collection** with `ImportError: cannot import name 'sample_windows'`.
This makes main's CI red on *every* recent commit (42a2e8ec, f3ffe31, …) — it is a
cc-transcript→cc-steer v10 migration gap, NOT a per-commit regression. Linux `ty`
still passes. Before assuming a push broke CI, check whether the failure is these
collection ImportErrors (pre-existing) vs. something new. The watcher daemon itself
is unaffected — it imports `cc_transcript.watch`/`activity`, not `mining`.

The shadow watcher is now supervised under launchd: `com.cc-steer.watch`
(KeepAlive+RunAtLoad), rendered by `cc_steer/launchd.py` (`render_watch`/
`install_watch`), wired into `cc-steer pipeline install-launchd --watch`. Restart /
model promotion = `launchctl kickstart -k gui/$(id -u)/com.cc-steer.watch`. The
Jul 2026 "watcher stall" was a false alarm: benign gate selectivity plus an earlier
pre-fix crash in the log ([[cc-pushback-two-stage-pipeline]]), not a live bug.
